title: Cosmos Remix — Harmonograph you can retune and reskin live
summary: Remixable particle harmonograph: particles trace a damped multi-pendulum Lissajous figure (x,y = Σ Aₖ·sin(fₖ·θ+φₖ)·e^{-decay·θ}) whose phases precess with time so the curve loops endlessly through elegant looping figures. Live glass panel: palette (brand theme tokens), shape, density, speed, dot size, plus Freq A and Freq B to retune the two driving pendulum frequencies. Reskins to any brand colour in one click. Self-contained, zero deps; the ?auto=1 capture hides the panel and renders a deterministic seamless loop.
usage_notes: RUN: open and use the panel (top-right) — pick a palette to reskin to any brand colour, drag Density/Spread/Speed/Dot size, set Freq A / Freq B to reshape the figure, press ↻ Seed to cycle palettes, ⧉ Copy to copy your config. KEY TECHNIQUES: a harmonograph is the sum of damped sinusoids on each axis — here each particle sits at a point along the pen path (θ swept across its index i), x and y are sums of sin(fₖ·θ+φ)·e^{-decay·θ} terms, and the phases φ advance with u so the whole figure precesses (seamless because all time enters via u·2π). Freq A / Freq B drive the two base frequencies; PARAMS convention = window.__params + setParam(key,value), the contract a generic remix harness reads to auto-build panels; theme tokens = a PALETTES map driving both a CSS accent var and the per-index colour ramp; SHAPES + drawShape render the chosen glyph cheaply and additively. CAPTURE: ?auto=1 hides the panel + window.__seek(t). TUNE: add palettes to PALETTES, raise count/dot for a denser field, push Freq A/B apart for busier curves.
